Did you know that the Russian word медведь и the English word honey
are actually connected through a very old shared idea?
They both come from ancient roots about sweetness.
This word started long ago as something like millet for honey.
It traveled through old languages and Russians created a polite nickname.
They didn't want to say the scary animals real ancient name out loud.

Russians have a very special relationship with bears.
In Russian fairy tales and folklore, the bear is often called Михаил Патапыч,
He is seen as strong but a bit clumsy and lovable, not just scary.
You will find bear statues, toys and even Olympic mascots all over Russia.
